flashcards.front: What is a class in Python?
flashcards.back: A blueprint for creating objects that defines attributes and methods common to all instances.

flashcards.front: Syntax to create a Python class

flashcards.back: Constructor method that initializes object attributes when a class instance is created.

flashcards.front: How to create a Sensor class instance?
flashcards.back: sensor1 = Sensor(name='DHT22', pin=4) # Assuming Sensor class exists
Creating a new class (child) that inherits attributes/methods from existing class (parent).
Bundling data (attributes) and methods that operate on data within a single unit (class).

flashcards.front: How to make private attributes in Python?
flashcards.back: Prefix with single underscore _ (convention) or double __ (name mangling).
Class: Shared by all instances (defined outside __init__). Instance: Unique to each object.
Reference to current instance, used to access attributes/methods within the class.
Different device classes (e.g., Sensor, Actuator) implement same method name with device-specific logic.

Use try-except blocks to handle errors in device communication:
try:
 sensor.read()
except IOError: ...
Calls parent class methods, essential in inheritance for initializing parent attributes.
Model physical devices as objects with state (attributes) and behavior (methods), enabling modular systems.
gpiozero (LED class), paho-mqtt (Client class), Adafruit_CircuitPython (I2CDevice class)
Prefer composition: Create device classes containing sensor/actuator objects rather than deep inheritance.
Static: @staticmethod, no cls param. Class: @classmethod, cls param. Static methods are utility functions.
1. Code reusability 2. Modular design 3. Easier maintenance 4. Real-world modeling 5. Encapsulation of device logic
